title: Advanced connectors and discourse markers
lesson: Connectors differ in grammar as well as meaning. 'Although/though/even though' are conjunctions and introduce a clause (subject + verb); 'despite/in spite of' are prepositions and take a noun, pronoun or '-ing' form. Contrast across sentences uses adverbs like 'however' and 'nevertheless' (often with a comma). 'Whereas' marks direct contrast between two facts. For cause, 'owing to' and 'due to' take a noun phrase, while 'hence' and 'thus' introduce a consequence. 'Provided (that)' sets a condition; 'in order to' and 'so as to' express purpose before an infinitive; 'no matter how/what' concedes regardless of degree.

items.text: {1} the heavy rain, the match went ahead as planned.
items.explanation: 'Despite' is a preposition and is followed by a noun phrase ('the heavy rain'). 'Although' would need a clause; 'however' is an adverb; 'whereas' marks contrast between two facts.

items.text: {1} it was raining heavily, the match went ahead as planned.
items.explanation: 'Although' is a conjunction introducing a full clause ('it was raining'). 'Despite' and 'in spite of' need a noun, not a clause; 'hence' shows consequence.

items.text: My brother is very outgoing, {1} I tend to be rather shy.
items.explanation: 'Whereas' draws a direct contrast between two simultaneous facts (outgoing vs shy). The other options express cause, concession with a noun, or purpose.

items.text: The figures look discouraging. {1}, there are real grounds for optimism.
items.explanation: 'Nevertheless' is a concessive adverb meaning 'in spite of that', linking two sentences. 'Therefore' shows result; 'owing to' needs a noun; 'provided that' sets a condition.

items.text: The plan is ambitious. It is, {1}, entirely achievable.

blanks.hint: contrast adverb, here placed mid-sentence between commas

items.explanation: 'However' is a contrast adverb that can sit mid-sentence between commas. 'Hence' signals consequence; 'whereas' joins clauses; 'in order to' expresses purpose.

items.text: The flight was cancelled {1} the dense fog over the airport.
items.explanation: 'Owing to' is a prepositional phrase of cause followed by a noun phrase ('the dense fog'). 'Although' and 'whereas' need clauses; 'thus' introduces a consequence.

items.text: The road was closed {1} an accident earlier that morning.
items.explanation: 'Due to' introduces a cause as a noun phrase ('an accident'). 'So that' introduces a purpose clause; 'nevertheless' is concessive; 'no matter how' concedes degree.

items.text: You may borrow the car {1} you return it by six.
items.explanation: 'Provided that' introduces a condition ('if and only if you return it by six'). The alternatives express concession with a noun, consequence, or contrast.

items.text: She left an hour early {1} avoid the rush-hour traffic.
items.explanation: 'In order to' expresses purpose and is followed by an infinitive ('avoid'). 'Owing to' and 'despite' take nouns; 'whereas' joins contrasting clauses.

items.text: He spoke slowly {1} make himself perfectly understood.
items.explanation: 'So as to' + infinitive expresses purpose, equivalent to 'in order to'. 'Due to' takes a noun; 'however' is a contrast adverb; 'provided that' is conditional.

items.text: The evidence was inconclusive; {1}, the case was dismissed.
items.explanation: 'Hence' introduces a logical consequence ('for this reason'). The other options express concession or contrast, not result.

items.text: {1} hard she tried, she couldn't open the jar.
items.explanation: 'No matter how + adjective/adverb' concedes regardless of degree ('however hard'). It is followed by a clause; 'despite' and 'owing to' take nouns, and 'provided that' is conditional.
